reasoning: The resolution requires there to be more than 10 times the 'one plus the 30-day average' of Explosions/Remote violence in the 30 days preceding the resolution date. The most recent reference value for this threshold is 2.833, meaning the threshold for positive resolution is >28.33 (so 29 or more events). A reference value of ~2.833 implies there were only about 22 such events in the ENTIRE previous year (~1.833 events per 30 days). Achieving 29+ events in a single 30-day window would require a massive unforeseen conflict akin to a full-scale war (like the 2017 Marawi siege), producing more bombings and airstrikes in a month than typically happen all year. My recent searches found no evidence of any sudden major escalations of conflict, airstrikes, or terror campaigns in May or June 2025. Given the extreme rarity of such a spike, the probability for the immediate June 2025 and July 2025 resolution dates is practically zero (modeled at the minimum allowed bounds of 0.02-0.03). Probabilities slowly rise towards 0.05 for later 2025 dates to reflect growing time horizon uncertainty, but a 10x spike remains structurally extremely unlikely.
